title:
MID TERM EXAMINATION -FEBRUARY 2026
GRADE 9 - NATIONAL
subtitle: HEALTH AND PHYSICAL EDUCATION
instructions:
Read the questions carefully before answering the paper.
Write your name and the class in box at the top of the page.
Marks for each question are shown in brackets.

blocks.title: Underline the correct answer.
Who must pick up a dropped baton in a relay race?
If a pregnant woman does not receive enough nutrients the baby can be exposed to the following risks, select the WRONG one.
Why are small fish better for pregnant mothers?
According to nutritional guidelines, at what point should pregnant women be started on iron tablets? Select the correct one.
options: From the first week of pregnancy
Only after the baby is born

blocks.title: Regarding the daily energy requirement of a youth (adolescent), which of the following is correct?
What helps sports persons prevent muscle fatigue?
Baton change techniques are classified into two groups, identify the WRONG technique from the list below.
How is the hand held in the downsweep technique?
There are three specific techniques used in Long Jump, identify the one that is NOT a recognized technique.
How should vegans get essential amino acids?
Write short answers.
Write short answers.
How would you classify long jump according to the classification of athletic events.
blocks.answer: It is a horizontal jump field event.

blocks.title: What are the 3 techniques of long jump?
blocks.answer:
1. Hang technique
2. Sail technique
3. Hitch kick technique

blocks.title: What are the 4 phases of long jump?
blocks.answer:
1. Approach run
2. Take off
3. Flight
4. Landing

blocks.title: State 2 occasions which are considered as failures / foules in long jump.
blocks.markingInstructions:
Give marks for any similar answers. 
Refer to page 78 of the textbook.
blocks.answer:
1. Touching the ground beyond the take off  line with any part of the jumper’s body while taking off for the jump.
2. Taking of by placing the take off foot on the ground outside of the 2 sides of the take off board.

blocks.title: State 2 factors that are very important in relay races.
blocks.answer:
Maintaining a maximum running speed throughout the race.
Changing of the baton correctly and efficiently.

blocks.title: State the 2 important factors for a correct baton change.
blocks.answer:
1. Runners have a good training.
2. Good understanding among them.
blocks.markingInstructions: Give marks for any similar answers.

blocks.title: Fill in the blanks with the correct word.
blocks.answer:
A - Down sweep
B - Inside pass
C - Non-visual pass
blocks.markingInstructions: Only give marks if these exact words are used.

blocks.title: State the steps for practicing the baton change.
blocks.answer: Get into groups of 4 and stand in lines keeping sufficient gaps between two students/runners. Pass the baton from the back to the front and practice this posing it in the same place second by walking, third by running slowly and then running fast.
blocks.markingInstructions: Give marks for any similar answers.

blocks.title: Write short answers.
State 3 child and maternal health programs that have been implanted to reduce malnutrition in childhood.
blocks.answer:
1. Promotion of breastfeeding.
2. Giving adequate nutrition to sick children
3. Providing adequate and healthy supplementary food.
4. Monitoring growth.

blocks.title: Who is an adolescent?
blocks.answer: A child who is in the age group of 10 to 19 years
blocks.markingInstructions: Give marks for the age group.

blocks.title: What changes take place during this period?
blocks.answer: Accelerated growth in height and weight.

blocks.title: Write 4 factors that determine nutritional needs during this period?
blocks.answer:
Age
Gender 
Height
Weight
Level of Physical Activity
blocks.markingInstructions: For any 3 correct answers give 2 marks

blocks.title: Name 2 extra amounts of nutritional requirements needed during this phase.
blocks.answer:
Protein
Mineral Ions Fe, I2, Ca, 
Vitamins
Folic Acid

blocks.title: State 2 bad eating habits.
blocks.answer:
1. Missing breakfast
2. Eating junk food and fast food
3. Consuming sweet and fizzy drinks
4. Consuming alcohol

blocks.title: State 3 consequences of bad food habits during this phase.
blocks.answer:
1. Obesity
2. Deficiency of Micronutrients
3. More susceptible to develop non communicable diseases
blocks.markingInstructions: Give marks for any 3 similar answers.

blocks.title: State 3 groups of people that need a special diet.
blocks.answer:
1. Pregnant women
2. Breastfeeding mothers
3. Athletes
4. People who engage in strenuous physical activity

blocks.title: Give 2 suggestions to increase the nutritional value of the meals for your family.
blocks.markingInstructions: Give marks for any suitable answer.
